hypothesis: Self-supervised contrastive pretraining on a large unlabeled corpus of SEM images across magnifications and chemistries will produce representations that, after fine-tuning, require at most half the number of labeled additive-containing target samples to achieve 0.95 F1 on the nine-class NCM composition-and-state SEM classification task compared with models pretrained in a supervised manner on additive-free SEM images.
